core_promise: Import official Snapchat Memories into Apple Photos accurately, privately, and safely, while restoring dates, preserving available locations, and preventing duplicate chaos.

locked_decisions.future_extensibility: Core logic reusable by a future Mac target
locked_decisions.photos_access: Full .readWrite required; limited access is blocked
locked_decisions.processing: On-device only, no backend, no cloud, no account system
locked_decisions.zip_strategy: Stream from picked source; never copy full ZIP into sandbox
locked_decisions.album_strategy: One master album in v1, named 'Snapchat Memories'
locked_decisions.duplicate_deletion: Review-only; never auto-delete; delete actions are separate from keep/skip choices and require their own confirmation step

duplicate_flow_phases.pre_import_dedupe: Classify candidates and apply defaults (skip confirmed, queue suspected). Optional user review of suspected duplicates before import. Keep/skip decisions are non-destructive.
duplicate_flow_phases.post_import_review: After import, surface unresolved suspected duplicates and confirmed skipped items. User makes keep/skip decisions. No deletions in this phase.
duplicate_flow_phases.destructive_delete_review: Separate, explicit phase. Deletion of existing library assets requires its own confirmation step. Never bundled with keep/skip. No auto-delete under any circumstances.

locked_decisions.privacy_stance: No Snapchat login, no backend, no cloud, no media leaves device, explicit review before destructive actions

non_goals: Backend analytics on media or location data

modules.name: App
modules.role: Composition root — the only place that imports both AppCore protocols and infrastructure implementations to wire them together
responsibilities: Concrete dependency wiring (imports infrastructure packages and connects implementations to AppCore protocols)

modules.name: DomainModels
responsibilities: Pure shared models, enums, value types

ImportSession (must include security-scoped bookmark data or equivalent source re-acquisition metadata; must handle bookmark staleness / source-missing as recoverable error), ParsedSnapRecord, ImportAsset, DuplicateCandidate, LibraryAssetReference, ExportHealthReport, ImportSummary, ResolvedMetadata, DiagnosticEvent
ImportMode, ImportPhase, MetadataConfidence, DuplicateConfidence, ImportFailureReason, PermissionsState enums

modules.name: DesignSystem
responsibilities: Shared UI components: buttons, cards, rows, badges, state views
modules.visual_direction: Native iOS, calm, high-trust, utility-first. SF Pro + SF Symbols. Neutral system colors. Restrained blue/indigo accent.

modules.name: AppCore
modules.role: Thin contracts/protocol surface — must NOT become a god module
constraints: Must remain thin — protocols and lightweight abstractions only
Must not have unnecessary concrete coupling to infrastructure packages
The App target (composition root) owns concrete wiring, not AppCore

table_notes.library_asset_cache: Refreshable cache derived from PhotoKit, NOT canonical truth. May become stale if the user's Photos library changes outside the app (iCloud sync, deletions, additions from other apps). The dedupe engine must treat it as best-effort and support cache refresh when needed.

modules.name: ZipAccess
responsibilities: ZIP and folder opening via security-scoped URLs

modules.name: PhotosAccess
responsibilities: Library reads for dedupe candidate narrowing

modules.name: ImportEngine
modules.dependency_note: Consumes protocols defined in AppCore. Receives concrete implementations of ZipAccess, Persistence, PhotosAccess, DedupeEngine, Diagnostics via DI at the App composition root.

modules.name: DedupeEngine
modules.dependency_note: Consumes protocols defined in AppCore. Receives concrete implementations via DI.

modules.name: FeatureImportOptions
responsibilities: Best Effort vs Strict mode selection

screens.name: WelcomeScreen
screens.purpose: Explain app purpose, reinforce privacy, detect resumable sessions

screens.name: PermissionsScreen
screens.purpose: Request and enforce full Photos access, block limited access

screens.name: ImportSourceScreen
screens.purpose: Pick ZIP or folder, explain official export requirement

screens.name: ExportHealthScreen
screens.purpose: Show scan results, counts, health score, warnings

screens.name: ImportOptionsScreen
screens.purpose: Lock import mode, dedupe behavior, album destination

screens.name: ImportProgressScreen
screens.purpose: Show phase, progress, batch state, resumability

screens.name: DuplicateReviewScreen
screens.purpose: Review duplicates with deliberate user actions

screens.name: ImportSuccessScreen
screens.purpose: Summarize results, offer Photos and cleanup CTAs

assumptions.statement: The app product name is 'SnapOut'. Xcode project and target names will use 'SnapOut' rather than 'SnapImportApp'.
iOS 17.0 is the minimum deployment target, enabling @Observable, NavigationStack, and modern structured concurrency.
Single Xcode project with embedded local SPM packages in a Packages/ directory, not a multi-project workspace.
Each SPM package has its own test target. SharedTestSupport provides shared fixtures and mocks.
No CI/CD pipeline in v1. Local development and manual testing only.
Protocol-facing dependencies means the consuming module depends on protocol definitions and receives concrete implementations via DI, not direct module imports of infrastructure packages.
DiagnosticDetailsScreen is a secondary sub-screen accessible from Settings, not a primary navigation flow screen.
assumptions.source: Brief section 17 shell milestone screen list

assumptions.statement: All packages live in the same repository. Monorepo structure.

risks.title: Unknown Snapchat export format
risks.description: No sample export data is available yet. The parser is the spine of the pipeline but its concrete internals cannot be finalized without knowing the actual manifest JSON structure, field names, and folder hierarchy.
risks.not_blocked: Shell milestone, parser protocol interfaces, parser module scaffolding, overall pipeline architecture
risks.blocked: Real parser implementation (manifest decoding, field mapping, path resolution) and parser unit tests
risks.mitigation: Parser is behind protocol boundaries with a pluggable resolver chain. Internals will be built when sample data arrives.

risks.title: Security-scoped URL lifecycle management
risks.description: iOS security-scoped URLs require careful start/stop access management. App kill mid-session requires bookmark-based re-acquisition. Source file may be moved or deleted between sessions.
risks.mitigation: ZipAccess isolates this concern. Session persistence includes bookmark data. Resume flow handles lost source access.

risks.title: iCloud-backed library assets during dedupe
risks.description: PHAssets may have original data in iCloud, not locally available. Fingerprinting may trigger downloads, stall, or fail.
risks.mitigation: Degrade to suspected duplicate if exact verification cannot complete promptly. Allow deferred verification.

risks.title: 20 GB-scale import performance on device
risks.description: Staging, hashing, and writing 20 GB of media on a phone is resource-intensive. Memory pressure, storage pressure, and long sessions are concerns.
risks.mitigation: Batched execution, aggressive staged-file cleanup, dynamic batch sizing, persistent checkpointing.

risks.title: PhotoKit write reliability and crash-before-checkpoint
risks.description: PhotoKit write succeeds but app crashes before local DB update, creating ghost imports invisible to the resume engine.
risks.mitigation: Immediate checkpoint after PhotoKit success. Reconciliation step on resume. Small batch sizes to narrow the risk window.

risks.title: ZIPFoundation edge cases
risks.description: If Snapchat exports use password protection or edge-case ZIP features, ZIPFoundation may not handle them.
risks.mitigation: ZIP access is behind a protocol. Implementation is swappable.

risks.title: Large photo library indexing time
risks.description: Users with 50K+ assets will experience slow library indexing if done naively.
risks.mitigation: Use only coarse, cheap attributes for the index. Expensive fingerprints only on narrowed candidates. Show progress.

open_questions.question: What is the actual Snapchat export manifest structure (JSON schema, field names, folder hierarchy)?
open_questions.note: Shell milestone and parser interfaces are NOT blocked. Only real parser implementation and parser tests are blocked until sample data is available.

resolved_questions.question: What should the master album name be in Photos?
Should service protocols live in AppCore or be split across infrastructure packages?
resolved_questions.resolution: All service protocols centralized in AppCore. Infrastructure packages provide implementations. Feature packages depend on AppCore for their protocol surface.

resolved_questions.question: Should the resume flow appear as a modal on the Welcome screen or as a distinct screen in the navigation stack?
resolved_questions.resolution: Conditional card/banner on the Welcome screen. Tapping navigates into the appropriate resume point in the pipeline. No separate top-level screen.
